title: Docker apiversion needs to be updated for new fields like `start_interval` to work
body:
### Issue Description

Currently it is not possible to use fields like `start_interval`.
Podman fails with an error message like `can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later`. After further investigation we found out that the docker apiversion needs to be at least v1.44 but is instead v1.41.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create docker-compose file with health checks that have `start_period` and `start_interval`
2. run podman compose up


### Describe the results you received

I get the error message `can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later`

### Describe the results you expected

The containers should come up successfully with the `start_interval` working

comments.body: @inknos Did this one come up when you looked at newer Docker API versions?
It is tracked as part of v1.44 sync
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I can confirm the issue in the latest version: v1.21.0

The issue still persists in Podman 5.6.2:

```shell
core@coreos:~/podman/state-interval$ podman compose up

[+] Running 1/0
 ✔ Network state-interval_default  Created                                                     0.0s
 ⠋ Container state-interval-web-1  Creating                                                    0.0s
can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later
Error: executing /usr/local/bin/docker-compose up: exit status 1
```

Docker api is still on 1.4.1:

```shell
core@coreos:~/podman/state-interval$ curl --head --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock http://d/v5.6.2/_ping ; echo

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Api-Version: 1.41
Builder-Version: 1
Buildkit-Version:
Cache-Control: no-cache
Docker-Experimental: true
Libpod-Api-Version: 5.6.2
Libpod-Buildah-Version: 1.41.5
Ostype: linux
Pragma: no-cache
Server: Libpod/5.6.2 (linux)
X-Reference-Id: 0x40000dc950
Date: Sun, 09 Nov 2025 12:19:04 GMT
```

This issue is till relevant.  

My trace:
```
✘ Container minio               can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later                  0.1s 
✘ Container app                 can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later                  0.1s 
can't set healthcheck.start_interval as feature require Docker Engine v25 or later
make: *** [start-local] Error 1
```

I can't create containers using `volume.type=image` from a [compose file](https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/13547) using podman due to this issue:

```
Container cb volume with type=image require Docker Engine v28 or later 
volume with type=image require Docker Engine v28 or later
Error: executing /usr/local/bin/docker-compose --progress=plain up --quiet-build --quiet-pull --pull=always --build: exit status 1
```

```
/usr/local/bin/docker-compose --version                                            
Docker Compose version v5.0.2

podman --version                                            
podman version 5.7.1
```

Apparently this is possible when using [podman-compose](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/1202), but this is not included with Podman Desktop. I'm using the preinstalled Compose Extension v1.25.1.

Thanks for bumping the Docker-compatible API version to v1.44 in #27915.

I tested `healthcheck.start_interval` again with a recent Podman and Docker Compose provider. The previous API-version error is gone, but
`start_interval` still does not work. It is now silently ignored without a warning or error.

## Reproducer

```console
$ podman --version
podman version 5.8.2
$ podman compose version
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

Docker Compose version v5.3.1
```

```yaml
# compose.yaml
services:
  test:
    image: docker.io/library/busybox:latest
    container_name: health-start-interval-repro
    command: ["sh", "-c", "sleep 120"]
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - "date +%s >> /tmp/healthcheck-times; exit 1"
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 2s
      retries: 100
      start_period: 10s
      start_interval: 1s
```

```console
$ podman compose up -d
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

[+] up 2/2
 ✔ Network pc_test_default               Created                                                                                 0.0s
 ✔ Container health-start-interval-repro Started                                                                                 0.5s
$ sleep 12
$ podman exec health-start-interval-repro \
    sh -c 'cat /tmp/healthcheck-times; printf "count="; wc -l < /tmp/healthcheck-times'
1784071803
count=1
$ podman compose down
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

[+] down 2/2
 ✔ Container health-start-interval-repro Removed                                                                                11.8s
 ✔ Network pc_test_default               Removed                                                                                 0.0s
```

Because `start_period` is 10 seconds and `start_interval` is 1 second, I expected approximately ten healthcheck executions during the first ten seconds. Instead, only the initial healthcheck was executed. Subsequent checks use the normal 30-second `interval`.

## Source-code observation

The Docker-compatible container-create handler currently copies these fields from `Config.Healthcheck`:

- `Interval`
- `Retries`
- `StartPeriod`
- `Timeout`

but it does not copy `StartInterval`:

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/e3727b88b1ab9e1fc6cf2d0f17657d8c232c4cbc/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go#L597-L617

The Libpod runtime also creates the normal healthcheck timer from `HealthCheckConfig.Interval`; it does not select `StartInterval` while the
container is within `StartPeriod`:

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/e3727b88b1ab9e1fc6cf2d0f17657d8c232c4cbc/libpod/container_internal.go#L1125-L1131

Therefore, bumping the advertised compatibility API version to v1.44 removes Docker Compose's client-side validation error, but the received `Healthcheck.StartInterval` value is discarded by Podman and is not implemented by the runtime.

The original expected result in this issue says that containers should start with `start_interval` working. Should the remaining API and runtime implementation be tracked in this issue, or would you prefer a separate follow-up issue specifically for:

1. preserving `Healthcheck.StartInterval` in the compatibility API;
2. exposing/storing it in Podman's healthcheck configuration;
3. using it during `StartPeriod` and switching to `Interval` afterward; and
4. adding API and runtime tests to prevent silent ignoring?

I can open a separate issue and cross-reference this one if that is preferred.

For context, I have also filed a related issue in `podman-compose` about its current handling of `healthcheck.start_interval`:

https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/1500
